expectations.text: Develop a positive identity as a math learner and persevere when solving problems.
Read and represent whole numbers up to and including 50, and describe various ways they are used in everyday life.
Compose and decompose whole numbers up to and including 50 using a variety of tools and strategies.
Compare and order whole numbers up to and including 50.
Estimate the number of objects in collections of up to 50 and verify their estimates by counting.
Count to 50 by 1s, 2s, 5s, and 10s using a variety of tools and strategies.
Use drawings to represent and solve fair-sharing problems involving up to 10 items shared among 2–5 sharers.
Recognize that one half and two fourths of the same whole are equal in fair-sharing contexts.
Use drawings to compare and order unit fractions representing portions of a whole.
Use the properties of addition and subtraction to solve problems and check calculations.
Recall addition facts for numbers up to 10 and related subtraction facts.
Use mental math strategies to add and subtract numbers that total no more than 20.
Identify and describe repeating elements in patterns.
Determine pattern rules for repeating patterns.
Identify mathematical situations in everyday contexts.
Solve problems and represent mathematical situations using simple code involving sequential events.
Sort sets of data according to an attribute.
Collect data by asking counting questions.
Display data using graphs and pictographs.
Identify common two-dimensional shapes and three-dimensional objects.
Describe object locations using positional language.
Measure and record length, height, and distance.
Tell time to the hour and half-hour.
Identify Canadian coins and describe their value.
Develop confidence and perseverance when solving mathematical problems.
Read, represent, compose, and decompose whole numbers up to 200.
Compare and order whole numbers up to 200.
Use place value concepts to represent numbers.
Represent fractions as equal parts of a whole.
Add and subtract whole numbers up to 100.
Recall addition facts to 20 and related subtraction facts.
Use mental math strategies to solve problems.
Identify mathematical situations in real contexts.
Create and execute code involving sequential events.
Collect data through surveys and observations.
Identify attributes of shapes and objects.
Measure length and height using standard units.
Tell time to the quarter hour.
Identify Canadian coins and their value.
Apply social-emotional learning skills to mathematical learning.
Round numbers to the nearest ten or hundred.
Represent fractions as parts of a whole.
Add and subtract numbers up to 1000.
Solve problems involving multiplication and division.
Identify and describe repeating elements and operations in patterns.
Use algebraic reasoning to solve problems.
Write code using sequences and loops.
Measure length, perimeter, mass, and capacity.
Describe different ways people earn money.
Explain the difference between needs and wants.
Apply social-emotional learning skills to support mathematics learning.
Read, represent, and compare whole numbers up to 10 000.
Add and subtract numbers up to 10 000.
Write and execute code using loops.
Explain ways people earn and spend money.
Use social-emotional learning skills to support problem solving.
Read and represent numbers up to 100 000.
Compare and order decimals and fractions.
Write code using loops and conditional statements.
Use social-emotional learning skills to support mathematical thinking.
Read and represent numbers up to 1 000 000.
Compare and order fractions and decimals.
Represent relationships using variables and equations.
Write and execute code using loops and conditional logic.
